rows.source_ref: zeiman (Mantas, UK), "Trading System Simplicity", ForexFactory thread 1010582 (12 posts, n.d.), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1010582
rows.concept: In a 100-EMA-defined trend during the London session, enter on a Heiken Ashi candle color flip and scale out three equal-lot orders (two at 1:1, one trailed below each new HA candle).
rows.eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical HA color-change + 100EMA trend filter on FX majors H1 with stated SL (entry candle), TP=SL, 1% risk per order and HA-trailing exit — implementable in pure MQL5, FTMO/DXZ compatible.
rows.notes: Forum thread, only 12 posts. Author concedes heavy whipsaw/stop-outs in flat markets. Generic HA+EMA trend signal with weak/common edge; distinguishing features are the 06:00 GMT +8-9h session window and the A/B/C 3-order scale-out MM (borrowed from Peter Crown 'The Dibs'). Pairs: EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/CHF, USD/JPY. HA color is deterministic from HA open/close. Poster cfudge suggested an HA-histogram variant using the actual candle for SL/TP to survive flats.
